points.description: Plain citizens cannot understand bureaucratic form language — legal jargon, undefined acronyms, and passive-voice instructions create confusion and form abandonment
evidence: plainlanguage.gov research on government document comprehension

points.description: Multi-step forms with no visible progress indicator cause citizens to abandon submissions, not knowing how much remains or whether they can save and return
evidence: GOV.UK Design System research on form completion rates

points.description: Applications submitted with no status tracking leave citizens anxious, generating repeated phone calls and service desk load while eroding trust in government systems
evidence: USDS discovery research on benefits applications
GAO report on federal customer experience

points.description: Critical government documents distributed as untagged, inaccessible PDFs exclude screen reader users, people with cognitive disabilities, and mobile-only users
Citizens lose all entered form data when sessions expire without warning — especially harmful for complex benefit applications that may take 30–60 minutes to complete
Government forms designed only for desktop break on phones — fixed-width tables, tiny tap targets, and no mobile keyboard optimization exclude the 30%+ of citizens who are mobile-only
evidence: Pew Research mobile-only internet access data
